description: Relying on initial-block or power-up values for reset, which works in simulation and some FPGAs but not on ASICs.
body: Failure mode: registers depend on initial-block values or assumed power-up state for their reset value; this simulates fine and may work on FPGAs that load a bitstream, but ASIC flops power up in an unknown state. Detection signals: no explicit reset on some flops; X-propagation after reset in gate-level sim; design works in sim/FPGA but fails on ASIC. Do not use initial blocks for synthesizable reset. Safer fallback: drive an explicit reset to every state element, verify known state after reset deassert, and check gate-level reset behavior.
